company.name: Acme MX
company.seed_summary: Founder-led service business focused on sales clarity.

decision_question: What is the best Mexico-first move to convert more qualified founder-led service buyers without adding operational complexity too early?
current_thesis: Acme MX should lead with a tightly scoped sales clarity sprint because founder-led service buyers need proof, specificity, and a low-risk first engagement before they trust a broader advisory offer.
validated_facts: Founder-led service businesses react negatively to generic agency language.
The offer already has two visible proof points: transparent process and specific deliverables.
The current market model points to a meaningful SMB sales enablement segment in Mexico.
inferences: A focused first acquisition experiment is more likely to produce signal than broad-channel expansion.
The current target price can hold if scope and proof are made explicit in the sales conversation.
validation_gaps: The team still needs to verify whether buyers respond better to WhatsApp-led outreach or referral-led outreach.
The recommended price should be tested against live objections before any scale decision.
contrary_evidence: Some buyers may still see the sprint as too strategic unless the delivery mechanism is made concrete.
A competitor with more implementation proof could narrow differentiation quickly.
decision_criteria: Fastest route to credible market signal.
Trust fit for Mexico-based founder operators.
Execution simplicity in the first 90 days.
assumptions: The founder can personally own the first 30 days of validation and sales feedback collection.
The initial channel test can be run without adding paid media complexity.
execution_realities: The founder remains the primary seller and delivery lead during the first 90 days.
The system should favor sequential tests over parallel channel expansion.

options.label: Sales clarity sprint first
options.summary: Lead with the focused sprint, validate price and buyer trust, then expand only after signal improves.
pros: Fast path to market learning with low operational complexity.
Keeps the offer specific and easier to trust.
cons: Requires disciplined scope control so the sprint does not become custom consulting.

options.label: Broader advisory package first
options.summary: Sell a larger advisory engagement immediately to maximize revenue per client.
pros: Could raise revenue per win if buyers already trust the operator.
cons: Higher trust burden and more delivery complexity before enough proof exists.

icp.label: Founder-led service businesses
pains: Buyers do not understand what gets done.
desired_outcomes: Qualified conversations with the right buyers.

offer.name: Sales clarity sprint
offer.core_promise: Help founder-led service businesses avoid vague positioning and reach qualified conversations.
offer.mechanism: A founder-guided review of sales messaging, offer structure, and buyer objections.

key_assumptions: Annual price per customer assumed at MXN 36,000.
Target customer ratio set at 18% of the total addressable base.

decision_memo.recommended_action: Launch the first Mexico-first acquisition experiment on the best-fit channel.
decision_memo.why_now: The system has enough evidence and confidence to recommend a focused next move.
key_risks: Execution may still fail if the offer is not explained with enough specificity.
Channel assumptions should be validated with early conversion data.
next_steps: Launch the first Mexico-first acquisition experiment on the best-fit channel.
Test the target pricing tier with explicit scope and proof.
Measure conversion quality, not just lead volume, for 30 days.

milestones.name: Launch the first Mexico-first acquisition experiment on the best-fit channel.
milestones.success_metric: First execution launched with a clear message and tracking in place.

milestones.name: Test the target pricing tier with explicit scope and proof.
milestones.success_metric: Offer, pricing, and objections updated from live feedback.

milestones.name: Measure conversion quality, not just lead volume, for 30 days.
milestones.success_metric: Conversion quality measured and the next repeatable channel decision documented.
